concept: Compatible / Able to Work Together for a Stated Purpose
description: Able to work together for a stated purpose and under stated interface conditions. Compatible devices or formats need not be identical, accessible, or reliable outside that pairing; 'nowiso' (interface) names where interaction occurs, while 'thosami' says the selected parts can cooperate there.
sound_symbolism: Rounded 'tho' begins with breath along the teeth. Stressed 'sa' shifts that stream into a fine hiss and broad 'a'. Final 'mi' closes the lips around a bright nasal ending. Three places of contact meet cleanly, with the widest vowel held at the center.
grammatical_notes: A predicative or attributive adjective, often with a plural subject: 'lo keli thosami nai.' (The devices are compatible.) A 'nua' phrase may name the other component. The quality-noun rule supplies compatibility.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Open, repairable systems depend on parts that can meet across generations of tools instead of trapping every user inside one vendor or design.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Compatibility keeps a technical fit claim separate from the human questions of access, consent, and usefulness.

tags.activity: components working together for a selected purpose
tags.dialogue: communication formats that can be exchanged or rendered together
